hypothesis: Systems-optimizer ratchets become safer if every writable_scope is forced onto the explicit control-plane allowlist and rejected when it uses recursive `CLAW/control-plane/**/*` globs that can escape into runtime, queue, lock, or checkpoint surfaces.
measurement: Before the ratchet, systems-optimizer policy, lane writes, and backlog cards could still use recursive `CLAW/control-plane/**/*.json` or `/**/*.md` patterns, which are broader than the lane brief and can silently authorize runtime, queue, lock, or checkpoint writes. After the ratchet, `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs` rejects any lane, policy, or ratchet card whose writable_scope escapes the explicit allowlist or intersects a forbidden control-plane surface, and the fixed scope-local promotion bundle stays green.
keep_rule: Keep only if the verifier fails out-of-scope writable_scope entries while the lane contract, systems-optimizer state, and fixed scope-local evaluation bundle remain clean without touching route implementation paths.
learning_item: Systems-optimizer writable_scope must stay on the explicit control-plane allowlist; recursive control-plane globs are too broad because they can silently reach runtime and recovery state.
